Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: incorporates three or more closed or open loop servo-devices which may include stepping motors; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: has user-accessible programmability by means of teach/playback method or by means of an electronic computer which may be a programmable logic controlled, i.e., without mechanical intervention.
The above definition does not include the following devices:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: manipulation mechanisms which are only manually/teleoperator controllable;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: fixed sequence manipulation mechanisms which are automated moving devices operating according to mechanically fixed programmed motions. The program is mechanically limited by fixed stops, such as pins or cams. The sequence of motions and the selection of paths or angles are not variable or changeable by mechanical, electronic, or electrical means;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: mechanically controlled variable sequence manipulation mechanisms which are automated moving devices operating according to mechanically fixed programmed motions. The program is mechanically limited by fixed, but adjustable, stops such as pins or cams. The sequence of motions and the selection of paths or angles are variable within the fixed program pattern. Variations or modifications of the program pattern (e.g., changes of pins or exchanges of cams) in one or more motion axes are accomplished only through mechanical operations;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: non-servo-controlled variable sequence manipulation mechanisms which are automated moving devices, operating according to mechanically fixed programmed motions. The program is variable but the sequence proceeds only by the binary signal from mechanically fixed electrical binary devices or adjustable stops; or

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: stacker cranes defined as Cartesian coordinate manipulator systems manufactured as an integral part of a vertical array of storage bins and designed to access the contents of those bins for storage or retrieval.
End-effectors include grippers, active tooling units, and any other tooling that is attached to the baseplate on the end of a robot manipulator arm.
The definition in paragraph 1(a) does not include robots specially designed for non-nuclear industrial applications such as automobile paint-spraying booths.

Text.Emphasis: Vibration test systems, equipment, components and software therefor, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: electrodynamic vibration test systems, employing feedback or closed loop control techniques and incorporating a digital controller, capable of vibrating at 10 g RMS or more between 20 Hz and 2 000 Hz and capable of imparting forces of 50 kN measured bare table or greater;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: digital controllers, combined with specially designed software for vibration testing, with a real-time bandwidth greater than 5 kHz and being designed for use with the systems referred to in paragraph (a);

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: vibration thrusters (shaker units), with or without associated amplifiers, capable of imparting a force of 50 kN (11,250 lbs.), measured bare table, or greater, which are usable for the systems referred to in paragraph (a);

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: test piece support structures and electronic units designed to combine multiple shaker units into a complete shaker system capable of providing an effective combined force of 50 kN, measured bare table, or greater, which are usable for the systems referred to in paragraph (a); and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: specially designed software for use with the systems referred to in paragraph (a) or for the electronic units referred to in paragraph (d).

Text.Emphasis: Vacuum or other controlled atmosphere metallurgical melting and casting furnaces and related equipment, as follows, as well as specially configured computer control and monitoring systems and specially designed software for that purpose:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: arc remelt furnaces, arc melt furnaces and arc melt and casting furnaces with consumable electrode capacities between 1 000 cm3 and 20 000 cm3 and capable of operating with melting temperatures above 1 700°C;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: electron beam melting furnaces, plasma atomization furnaces and plasma melting furnaces with a power of 50 kW or greater and capable of operating with melting temperatures above 1 200°C;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: plasma torches specially designed for the furnaces specified in paragraph (b) having both of the following characteristics:
operating at a power greater than 50 kW; and
capable of operating above 1 200°C; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: electron beam guns specially designed for the furnaces specified in paragraph (b) operating at a power greater than 50 kW.

Text.Emphasis: Uranium isotope separation equipment and components (other than items listed in paragraph A.2.4.)

Provision.Label: B.2.2.1
Provision.Text: Electrolytic cells for fluorine production with a production capacity greater than 250 g of fluorine per hour.

Text.Emphasis: Rotor fabrication and assembly equipment and bellows-forming mandrels and dies, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: rotor assembly equipment for assembly of gas centrifuge rotor tube sections, baffles, and end caps. Such equipment includes precision mandrels, clamps, and shrink fit machines;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: rotor straightening equipment for alignment of gas centrifuge rotor tube sections to a common axis; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: bellows-forming mandrels and dies for producing single-convolution bellows (bellows made of high-strength aluminium alloys, maraging steel, or high-strength filamentary materials). The bellows have all of the following dimensions:
75 mm to 650 mm inside diameter;
12.7 mm or more in length;
single convolution depth of more than 2 mm.

Text.Emphasis: Centrifugal multiplane balancing machines, fixed or portable, horizontal or vertical, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: centrifugal balancing machines designed for balancing flexible rotors having a length of 600 mm or more and having all of the following characteristics:
a swing or journal diameter of 75 mm or more;
mass capability of from 0.9 kg to 23 kg (2 lbs. to 50 lbs.); and
capable of balancing speed of revolution of more than 5 000 rpm; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: centrifugal balancing machines designed for balancing hollow cylindrical rotor components and having all of the following characteristics:
a journal diameter of 75 mm or more;
mass capability of from 0.9 kg to 23 kg (2 lbs. to 50 lbs.);
capable of balancing to a residual imbalance of 0.010 kg mm/kg per plane or better; and
belt drive type;
and specially designed software therefor.

Text.Emphasis: Filament winding machines and related equipment, as follows, and specially designed software for those machines and related equipment:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: filament-winding machines having all of the following characteristics:
motions for positioning, wrapping and winding fibres coordinated and programmed in two or more axes;
specially designed to fabricate composite structures or laminates from fibrous or filamentary materials; and
capable of winding cylindrical tubes with an internal diameter between 75 mm and 650 mm and lengths of 300 mm or greater;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: coordinating and programming controls for the filament-winding machines specified in paragraph (a);

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: precision mandrels for the filament-winding machines specified in paragraph (a); and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: specially designed software for the filament-winding machines specified in paragraph (a).

Text.Emphasis: Frequency changers (also known as converters or inverters) or generators usable as a variable frequency or fixed frequency motor drive, and related software:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: frequency changers or generators having all of the following characteristics;
multiphase output capable of providing a power of 40 VA or greater;
operating at a frequency of 600 Hz or greater;
frequency control better (less) than 0.2%; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: software, as the case may be
specially designed for the use of equipment specified in paragraphs (a)(1) to (3);

having encryption keys or codes specially designed to enhance or release the performance characteristics of equipment not included in paragraphs (a)(1) to (3) so that it meets or exceeds the characteristics specified in those paragraphs; or
specially designed to enhance or release the performance characteristics of the equipment.
NOTE

Paragraph B.2.2.5 includes frequency changers intended for specific industrial machinery or consumer goods (machine tools, vehicles, etc.) if the frequency changers can meet the characteristics above when removed.

Text.Emphasis: Lasers, laser amplifiers, and oscillators as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: copper vapour lasers with 30 W or greater average output power operating at wavelengths between 500 nm and 600 nm;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: argon ion lasers with greater than 40 W average output power operating at wavelengths between 400 nm and 515 nm;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: neodymium-doped (other than glass) lasers with an output wavelength of between 1 000 nm and 1 100 nm having either of the following characteristics:
pulse-excited and Q-switched with a pulse duration equal to or greater than 1 ns, and having either of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: single-transverse mode output having an average output power exceeding 40 W; or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: multiple-transverse mode output having an average output power exceeding 50 W; or
frequency doubling incorporated to give an output wavelength between 500 nm and 550 nm with an average output power of greater than 40 W;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: tunable pulsed single-mode dye laser oscillators having all of the following characteristics:
operation at wavelengths of between 300 nm and 800 nm;
average output power greater than 1 W;
repetition rate greater than 1 kHz; and
pulse width less than 100 ns;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: tunable pulsed dye laser amplifiers and oscillators, except single mode oscillators, having all of the following characteristics:
operation at wavelengths of between 300 nm and 800 nm;
average output power greater than 30 W;
repetition rate greater than 1 kHz; and
pulse width less than 100 ns;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: alexandrite lasers having all of the following characteristics:
operation at wavelengths of between 720 nm and 800 nm;
average output power greater than 30 W;
repetition rate greater than 125 Hz; and
bandwidth of 0.005 nm or less;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: pulsed carbon dioxide lasers having all of the following characteristics:
operation at wavelengths of between 9 000 nm and 11 000 nm;
average output power greater than 500 W;
repetition rate greater than 250 Hz; and
pulse width less than 200 ns;
NOTE

Paragraph B.2.2.6.(g) does not include the higher power (typically 1 kW to 5 kW) industrial CO2 lasers used in applications such as cutting and welding, as those lasers are either continuous wave or are pulsed with a pulse width of more than 200 ns.

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: pulsed excimer lasers (XeF, XeCl, KrF) having all of the following characteristics:
operation at wavelengths of between 240 nm and 360 nm;
average output power greater than 500 W; and
repetition rate greater than 250 Hz;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: para-hydrogen Raman shifters designed to operate at 16 µm output wavelength and at a repetition rate greater than 250 Hz; and

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: pulsed carbon monoxide lasers having all of the following characteristics:
operating at wavelengths between 5 000 nm and 6 000 nm;
an average output power greater than 200 W;
a repetition rate greater than 250 Hz; and
pulse width of less than 200 ns.

Text.Emphasis: Mass spectrometers capable of measuring ions of 230 atomic mass units or greater with a resolution of better than 2 parts in 230, and one of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometers (ICP/MS);

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: glow discharge mass spectrometers (GDMS);

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: thermal ionization mass spectrometers (TIMS);

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: electron bombardment mass spectrometers having both of the following characteristics:
a molecular beam inlet system that injects a collimated beam of analyte molecules into a region of the ion source where the molecules are ionized by an electron beam; and
one or more cold traps that can be cooled to a temperature of 193 K (-80°C) or less in order to trap analyte molecules that are not ionized by the electron beam; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: mass spectrometers equipped with a microfluorination ion source designed for actinides or actinide fluorides.

Text.Emphasis: All types of pressure transducers capable of measuring absolute pressure and having the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: pressure sensing elements made of or protected by aluminium, aluminium alloy, aluminium oxide (alumina or sapphire), nickel or nickel alloys containing more than 60% nickel by weight, or fully fluorinated hydrocarbon polymers;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: seals, if any, essential for sealing the pressure sensing element, and in direct contact with the process medium, made of or protected by aluminium, aluminium alloy, aluminium oxide (alumina or sapphire), nickel or nickel alloys containing more than 60% nickel by weight, or fully fluorinated hydrocarbon polymers;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: either of the following characteristics:
a full scale of less than 13 kPa and an accuracy of better than ±1% of full scale; or
a full scale of 13 kPa or greater and an accuracy of better than ±130 Pa when measuring at 13 kPa.
NOTE
Pressure transducers are devices that convert pressure measurements into an electrical signal.
For the purpose of this paragraph, accuracy includes non-linearity, hysteresis and repeatability at ambient temperature.

Provision.Label: B.2.2.9
Provision.Text: Valves 5 mm (0.2 in.) or greater in nominal size, with a bellows seal, wholly made of or lined with aluminium, aluminium alloy, nickel, or alloy containing 60% or more nickel, either manually or automatically operated.
NOTE
For valves with different inlet and outlet diameters, the nominal size parameter above refers to the smallest diameter.

Text.Emphasis: Superconducting solenoidal electromagnets with all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: capable of creating magnetic fields of more than 2 T (20 kilogauss);

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: with an L/D (length divided by inner diameter) greater than 2;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: with an inner diameter of more than 300 mm; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: with a magnetic field uniform to better than 1% over the central 50% of the inner volume.
NOTE

This paragraph does not cover magnets specially designed for and exported as parts of medical nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) imaging systems. It is understood that the wording “as part of” does not necessarily mean physical part in the same shipment. Separate shipments from different sources are allowed, provided that the related export documents clearly specify the “part of” relationship.

Provision.Label: B.2.2.11
Provision.Text: Vacuum pumps with an input throat size of 38 cm (15 in.) or greater with a pumping speed of 15,000 L/s or greater and capable of producing an ultimate vacuum better than 104 torrs (1.33 x 10-4 mbar).
NOTE
The ultimate vacuum is determined at the input of the pump with the input of the pump blocked off.
The pumping speed is determined at the measurement point with nitrogen gas or air.

Provision.Label: B.2.2.12
Provision.Text: Direct current high-power supplies capable of continuously producing, over a time period of 8 hours, 100 V or greater with current output of 500 amps or greater and with current or voltage regulation better than 0.1%.

Provision.Label: B.2.2.13
Provision.Text: High-voltage direct current power supplies capable of continuously producing, over a time period of 8 hours, 20 000 V or greater with current output of 1 amp or greater and with current or voltage regulation better than 0.1%.

Provision.Label: B.2.2.14
Provision.Text: Electromagnetic isotope separators, designed for or equipped with, single or multiple ion sources capable of providing a total ion beam current of 50 mA or greater.
NOTES

This paragraph includes separators capable of enriching stable isotopes as well as those for uranium. A separator capable of separating the isotopes of lead with a one-mass unit difference is inherently capable of enriching the isotope of uranium with three-unit mass difference.
This paragraph includes separators with the ion sources and collectors both in the magnetic field and those configurations in which they are external to the field.
A single 50 mA ion source will produce less than 3 g of separated HEU per year from natural abundance feed.

Text.Emphasis: Bellows-sealed scroll-type compressors and bellows-sealed scroll-type vacuum pumps having all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: capable of an inlet volume flow rate of 50 m3/h or greater;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: capable of a pressure ratio of 2:1 or greater; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: having all surfaces that come in contact with the process gas made from any of the following materials:
aluminium or aluminium alloy;
aluminium oxide;
stainless steel;
nickel or nickel alloy;
phosphor bronze; or
fluoropolymers.
NOTES

In a scroll-type compressor or vacuum pump, crescent-shaped pockets of gas are trapped between one or more pairs of intermeshed spiral vanes, or scrolls, one of which moves while the other remains stationary. The moving scroll orbits the stationary scroll and does not rotate. As the moving scroll orbits the stationary scroll, the gas pockets diminish in size (i.e., they are compressed) as they move toward the outlet port of the machine.

In a bellows-sealed scroll-type compressor or vacuum pump, the process gas is totally isolated from the lubricated parts of the pump and from the external atmosphere by a metal bellows. One end of the bellows is attached to the moving scroll and the other end is attached to the stationary housing of the pump.
Fluoropolymers include, but are not limited to, the following materials:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE);

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: fluorinated ethylene propylene (FEP);

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: perfluoroalkoxy (PFA);

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: polychlorotrifluoroethylene (PCTFE); and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: vinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene copolymer.

Text.Emphasis: Heavy water production plant-related equipment (other than items listed in paragraph A.2.5.).

Provision.Label: B.2.3.1
Provision.Text: Specialized packings for use in separating heavy water from ordinary water and made of phosphor bronze mesh (chemically treated to improve wettability) and designed for use in vacuum distillation towers.

Provision.Label: B.2.3.2
Provision.Text: Pumps circulating solutions of diluted or concentrated potassium amide catalyst in liquid ammonia (KNH2/NH3), with all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: airtight (i.e., hermetically sealed);

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: for concentrated potassium amide solutions (1% or greater), operating pressure of 1.5 MPa to 60 MPa (15 to 600 atmospheres); for dilute potassium amide solutions (less than 1%), operating pressure of 20 MPa to 60 MPa (200 to 600 atmospheres); and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: a capacity greater than 8.5 m3/h (5 cu.ft./ min.).

Text.Emphasis: Hydrogen-cryogenic distillation columns having all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: designed for operation at internal temperatures of 35 K (-238ºC) or less;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: designed for operation at internal pressures of 0.5 MPa to 5 MPa;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: constructed of either
stainless steels of SAE 300 series with low sulfur content and an austenitic ASTM (or equivalent standard) grain size number of 5 or greater; or
equivalent materials that are both cryogenic and H2-compatible; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: having internal diameters of 30 cm or greater and effective lengths of 4 m or greater.

Provision.Label: B.2.3.6
Provision.Text: Turboexpanders or turboexpander-compressor sets designed for operation below 35 K and a throughput of hydrogen gas of 1 000 kg/h or greater.

Text.Emphasis: Implosion systems development equipment

Flash x-ray generators or pulsed electron accelerators with peak energy of 500 keV or greater, as follows, except accelerators that are component parts of devices designed for purposes other than electron beam or x-ray radiation (electron microscopy, for example) and those designed for medical purposes:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: having an accelerator peak electron energy of 500 keV or greater but less than 25 MeV and with a figure of merit (K) of 0.25 or greater, where K is defined as:

FormulaGroup.FormulaConnector: where V is the peak electron energy in million electron volts and Q is the total accelerated charge in coulombs if the accelerator beam pulse duration is less than or equal to 1 µs, if the acceleration beam pulse duration is greater than 1 µs, Q is the maximum accelerated charge in 1 µs [Q equals the integral of i with respect to t, over the lesser of 1 µs or the time duration of the beam pulse (Q =ƒidt), where i is beam current in amperes and t is the time in seconds]; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: having an accelerator peak electron energy of 25 MeV or greater and a peak power greater than 50 MW. [Peak power = (peak potential in volts) x (peak beam current in amperes).]
NOTES

Time duration of the beam pulse means, in machines, based on microwave accelerating cavities, the time duration of the beam pulse is the lesser of 1 µs or the duration of the bunched beam packet resulting from one microwave modulator pulse.
Peak beam current means, in machines, based on microwave accelerating cavities, the peak beam current is the average current in the time duration of a bunched beam packet.

Provision.Label: B.2.4.2
Provision.Text: High-velocity gun systems (propellant, gas, coil, electromagnetic, and electrothermal types, and other advanced systems) capable of accelerating projectiles to 1.5 km/s or greater.
NOTE
Paragraph B.2.4.2 does not control guns specially designed for high-velocity weapon systems.

Text.Emphasis: High-speed cameras and imaging devices, and components for those cameras or devices, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: streak cameras, and specially designed components for those cameras, as follows:
streak cameras with writing speeds greater than 0.5 mm/µs;
electronic streak cameras capable of 50 ns or less time resolution;
streak tubes for cameras specified in paragraph (2);
plug-ins specially designed for use with streak cameras having modular structures and that enable the performance specifications set out in paragraphs (1) and (2); and
synchronizing electronic units, and their rotor assemblies consisting of turbines, mirrors and bearings, specially designed for cameras specified in paragraph (1);

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: framing cameras, and specially designed components for those cameras, as follows:
framing cameras with recording rates greater than 225 000 frames per second;
framing cameras capable of 50 ns or less frame exposure time;
framing tubes and solid-state imaging devices having a fast image gating (shutter) time of 50 ns or less specifically designed for cameras specified in paragraphs (1) and (2);
plug-ins specially designed for use with framing cameras having modular structures and that enable the performance specifications set out in paragraphs (1) and (2); and
synchronizing electronic units, and their rotor assemblies consisting of turbines, mirrors and bearings, specially designed for cameras specified in paragraphs (1) and (2); and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: solid-state or electron tube cameras and specially designed components for those cameras, as follows:
solid-state cameras or electron tube cameras, with a fast-image gating (shutter) time of 50 ns or less;
solid-state imaging devices and image intensifier tubes having a fast-image gating (shutter) time of 50 ns or less specially designed for cameras specified in paragraph (1);
electro-optical shutters (Kerr or Pockels cells) with a fast-image gating (shutter) time of 50 ns or less; and
plug-ins specially designed for use with cameras having modular structures and that enable the performance specifications set out in paragraph (1).

Text.Emphasis: Specialized instrumentation for hydrodynamic experiments, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: velocity interferometers for measuring velocities exceeding 1 km/s during time intervals of less than 10 µs;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: shock pressure gauges capable of measuring pressures greater than 10 GPa, including gauges made with manganin, ytterbium and polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) or polyvinyl difluoride (PVF2); and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: quartz pressure transducers for pressures greater than 10 GPa.
NOTE

Paragraph B.2.4.5(a) includes velocity interferometers such as velocity interferometer systems for any reflector (VISARs), Doppler laser interferometers (DLIs) and photonic Doppler velocimeters (PDVs), also known as heterodyne velocimeters (Het-V).

Text.Emphasis: High-explosive containment vessels, chambers, containers and other similar containment devices designed for the testing of high explosives or explosive devices and having both of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: designed to fully contain an explosion equivalent to 2 kg of trinitrotoluene (TNT) or greater; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: having design elements or features enabling real-time or delayed transfer of diagnostic or measurement information.

Text.Emphasis: Explosives and related equipment
Detonators and multipoint initiation systems (exploding bridge wire, slappers etc.):

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: electrically driven explosive detonators, as follows:
exploding bridge (EB);
exploding bridge wire (EBW);
slapper; and
exploding foil initiators (EFI); and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: arrangements using single or multiple detonators designed to nearly simultaneously initiate an explosive surface (over greater than 5 000 mm2) from a single firing signal (with an initiation timing spread over the surface of less than 2.5 µs).
NOTE

The detonators of concern all utilize a small electrical conductor (bridge, bridge wire or foil) that explosively vaporizes when a fast, high-current electrical pulse is passed through it. In nonslapper types, the exploding conductor starts a chemical detonation in a contacting high-explosive material such as PETN (pentaerythritoltetranitrate). In slapper detonators, the explosive vaporization of the electrical conductor drives a “flyer” or “slapper” across a chemical detonation. The slapper in some designs is driven by magnetic force. The term “exploding foil” detonator may refer to either an EB or a slapper-type detonator. Also, the word “initiator” is sometimes used in place of the word “detonator”.
Detonators using only primary explosives, such as lead azide, are not subject to control.

Text.Emphasis: Electronic components for firing sets (switching devices and pulse discharge capacitors):

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: switching devices:

cold-cathode tubes (including gas krytron tubes and vacuum sprytron tubes), whether gas filled or not, operating similarly to a spark gap, containing three or more electrodes, and having all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: anode peak voltage rating of 2 500 V or more;

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: anode peak current rating of 100 A or more; and

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: anode delay time of 10 µs or less;
triggered spark-gaps having an anode delay time of 15 µs or less and rated for a peak current of 500 A or more; and
modules or assemblies with a fast switching function having all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: anode peak voltage rating greater than 2 000 V;

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: anode peak current rating of 500 A or more; and

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: turn-on time of 1 µs or less; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: capacitors with the following characteristics:
voltage rating greater than 1.4 kV, energy storage greater than 10 J, capacitance greater than 0.5 µF, and series inductance less than 50 nH; or
voltage rating greater than 750 V, capacitance greater than 0.25 µF, and series inductance less than 10 nH.

Text.Emphasis: Firing sets and equivalent high-current pulse generators, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: detonator firing sets (initiation systems, firesets), including electronically charged, explosively driven and optically driven firing sets designed to drive multiple controlled detonators specified in paragraph B.2.5.1;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: modular electrical pulse generators (pulsers) having all of the following characteristics:
capable of delivering their energy in less than 15 µs into loads of less than 40 Ω;
having an output greater than 100 A;
designed for portable, mobile or ruggedized use;
having no dimension greater than 30 cm;
weighing less than 30 kg; and
specified to operate over an extended temperature range from 223 K to 373 K (-50°C to 100°C) or specified as suitable for aerospace applications; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: micro-firing units having all of the following characteristics:
no dimension greater than 35 mm;
voltage rating of equal to or greater than 1 kV;
capacitance of equal to or greater than 100 nF.
NOTE

Optically driven firing sets include both those employing laser initiation and laser charging. Explosively driven firing sets include both explosive ferroelectric and explosive ferromagnetic firing set types. Paragraph B.2.5.3(b) includes xenon flashlamp drivers.

Text.Emphasis: High explosive substances or mixtures containing more than 2% by weight of any of the following:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: cyclotetramethylenetetranitramine (HMX) (CAS 2691-41-0);

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: cyclotrimethylenetrinitramine (RDX) (CAS 121-82-4);

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: triaminotrinitrobenzene (TATB) (CAS 3058-38-6);

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: aminodinitrobenzo-furoxan or 7-amino-4,6 nitrobenzofurazane-1-oxide (ADNBF) (CAS 97096-78-1);

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: 1,1-diamino-2,2-dinitroethylene (DADE or FOX7) (CAS 145250-81-3);

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: 2,4-dinitroimidazole (DNI) (CAS 5213-49-0);

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: diaminoazoxyfurazan (DAAOF or DAAF) (CAS 78644-89-0);

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: diaminotrinitrobenzene (DATB) (CAS 1630-08-6);

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: dinitroglycoluril (DNGU or DINGU) (CAS 55510-04-8);

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: 2,6-Bis(picrylamino)-3,5-dinitropyridine (PYX) (CAS 38082-89-2);

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: 3,3′-diamino-2,2′,4,4′,6,6′-hexanitrobiphenyl or dipicramide (DIPAM) (CAS 17215-44-0);

Provision.Label: (l)
Provision.Text: diaminoazofurazan (DAAzF) (CAS 78644-90-3);

Provision.Label: (m)
Provision.Text: 1,4,5,8-tetranitro-pyridazino[4,5-d]pyridazine (TNP) (CAS 229176-04-9);

Provision.Label: (n)
Provision.Text: hexanitrostilbene (HNS) (CAS 20062-22-0) or;

Provision.Label: (o)
Provision.Text: any explosive with a crystal density greater than 1.8 g/cm3 and having a detonation velocity greater than 8 000 m/s.

Text.Emphasis: Striplines to provide a low inductance path to detonators and having both of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: voltage rating greater than 2 kV; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: inductance of less than 20 nH.

Text.Emphasis: Nuclear testing equipment and components

Provision.Label: B.2.6.1
Provision.Text: Photomultiplier tubes with a photocathode area greater than 20 cm2 having an anode pulse rise time of less than 1 ns.

Provision.Label: B.2.6.2
Provision.Text: High-speed pulse generators and pulse heads with output voltages greater than 6 V into a resistive load of less than 55 Ω and with pulse transition times of less than 500 ps (defined as the time interval between 10% and 90% voltage amplitude).

Text.Emphasis: Other equipment
Neutron generator systems, including tubes, having all of the following characteristics:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: designed for operation without an external vacuum system; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: utilizing electrostatic acceleration to induce
a tritium-deuterium nuclear reaction; or
a deuterium-deuterium nuclear reaction capable of an output of 3 × 109 neutrons/s or greater.

Text.Emphasis: Equipment related to nuclear material handling and processing and to nuclear reactors, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: remote manipulators that can be used to provide remote actions in radiochemical separation operations or hot cells, as follows:
having a capability of penetrating 0.6 m or more of hot cell wall (through-the-wall operation); or
having a capability of bridging over the top of a hot cell wall with a thickness of 0.6 m or more (over-the-wall operation);
NOTE
Remote manipulators provide translation of human operator actions to a remote operating arm and terminal fixture. They may be of a “master/slave” type or operated by joystick or keypad.

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: high-density (lead glass or other) radiation shielding windows greater than 0.09 m2 on cold area and with a density greater than 3 g/cm3 and a thickness of 100 mm or greater; and specially designed frames therefor; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: radiation-hardened TV cameras, or lenses therefor, specially designed or rated as radiation hardened to withstand greater than 5 x 104 Gy (Silicon) (5 x 106 rad (Silicon)) without operational degradation.

Text.Emphasis: Tritium facilities, plants and equipment, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: facilities or plants for the production, recovery, extraction, concentration or handling of tritium, tritium compounds or mixtures containing tritium; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: equipment for those facilities or plants as follows:
hydrogen or helium refrigeration units capable of cooling to 23 K (-250°C) or less, with heat removal capacity greater than 150 W; and
hydrogen isotope storage or hydrogen isotope purification systems using metal hydrides as the storage or purification medium.

Provision.Label: B.2.7.4
Provision.Text: Platinized catalysts specially designed or prepared for promoting the hydrogen isotope exchange reaction between hydrogen and water for the recovery of tritium from heavy water or for the production of heavy water.

Text.Emphasis: Lithium isotope separation facilities or plants and systems and equipment for that purpose, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: facilities or plants for the separation of lithium isotopes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: equipment for the separation of lithium isotopes based on the lithium-mercury amalgam process, as follows:
packed liquid-liquid exchange columns specially designed for lithium amalgams;
mercury and lithium amalgam pumps;
lithium amalgam electrolysis cells; or
evaporators for concentrated lithium hydroxide solution;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: ion exchange systems specially designed for lithium isotope separation and specially designed components for those systems; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: chemical exchange systems (employing crown ethers, cryptands or lariat ethers) specially designed for lithium isotope separation and specially designed components for those systems.

Text.Emphasis: Target assemblies and components for the production of tritium, as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: target assemblies, made of or containing lithium enriched in the lithium-6 isotope, specially designed for the production of tritium through irradiation, including insertion in a nuclear reactor; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: components specially designed for the target assemblies specified in paragraph (a).
NOTE
Components specially designed for target assemblies for the production of tritium may include lithium pellets, tritium getters and specially coated cladding.

Provision.Label: B.2.8
Provision.Text: Any equipment not otherwise included in paragraph B.2 if the equipment is intended, or there are reasonable grounds to suspect that it is intended, in whole or in part, for use in connection with the design, development, production, handling, operation, maintenance or storage of nuclear weapons or other nuclear explosive devices.

Text.Emphasis: Technology

Provision.Text: Technical data for the design, production, construction, operation or maintenance of any item in this Part, including, but not limited to, blueprints, plans, diagrams, models, formulae, engineering designs and specifications, software, manuals and instructions, except data available to the public (e.g., publications, publicly available websites or that which has been made available without restrictions on its further dissemination).
NOTE
Technical data referred to in paragraph B.3.1 is subject to control under both tangible and intangible modes of transfer.

Provision.Label: B.3.2
Provision.Text: Any information not otherwise included in paragraph B.3.1 if the information is intended, or there are reasonable grounds to suspect that it is intended, in whole or in part, for use in connection with the design, development, production, handling, operation, maintenance or storage of nuclear weapons or other nuclear explosive devices.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(1)(a) of the Nuclear Non-proliferation Import and Export Control Regulations2 is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant’s business number assigned by the Canada Revenue Agency, if applicable, their name, Canadian address, email address and telephone number;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (g), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (h) and by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant’s written process for the import or export of the substance, equipment or information.

Section.Text: The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 3:

Every person must retain, for a period of six years after the day on which a licence to import or export issued to them expires, the licence and all records relevant to any import or export under it, including, if applicable,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the application for the licence and supporting information submitted to the Commission or any designated officer who is authorized to carry out the duties set out in paragraphs 37(2)(c) and (d) of the Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the customs declaration and associated documentation submitted at the time of import or export;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shipping manifests and associated documentation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any purchase order and certificate of manufacture; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the notifications and other reporting submissions made under the licence.
